title: caryocar glabrum subsp. album
intro: Caryocar glabrum subsp. album, also known as white guanandi, is a plant species belonging to the Caryocar genus. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can be attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting immune function. In herbal preparations, Caryocar glabrum subsp. album is often used in teas, infusions, and extracts, and its leaves and bark are commonly used in traditional medicine to create remedies for various ailments.
